what_it_does: Maintains a persistent ingestion ledger for a 2026-06-12 reading run across ConceptV source documents, recording corpus status, page-based evidence, new design/code substrates, presentation findings, and prediction verdicts.
observations: Defines a 160-page, 12-document corpus with slug, source path, page count, date, and read status.
States that evidence lands in this ledger before decomposition into other faces.
Records product-document findings as new organisms, atoms, chrome, colour, voice, sequence, motion, application, and scale evidence.
Uses page-local citations such as vt-res p1, vt-arch p3, and landing p2 to anchor claims.
Tracks repeated design substrates including phase strips, process steps, stage cards, comparison matrices, testimonials, chips, CTAs, and logo strips.
Separates marketing, investor-deck, product, and presentation registers through observed voice, colour, and sequence behavior.
Records document-level reads for one-pagers, 15-page decks, 17-page pitch decks, 18-page company info, sampled 30-page capital raise material, and probed 49-page recent pitches.
Includes a judged predictions ledger with confirmed, supported, partial-miss, and nuance outcomes.
Ends with an estate-vs-corpus decomposition placeholder for token, registry entry, rule, and invariant records.
standouts: The phase_strip is described as a recurring document-internal map whose active chevron advances by page.
The file treats colour, shape, motion, voice, and sequence as evidence-bearing substrates rather than decoration.
The one-pager finding adds an extract strategy beside fusion for tier behavior.
The ledger records that product surfaces include distinct grounds, including tour canvas, captures ledger, and dashboard.
conventions: Uses markdown headings, tables, numbered findings, and dense bullet ledgers.
Names reusable substrates in snake_case or lower-case labels, then defines them with observed behavior and page evidence.
Uses uppercase emphasis for verdicts, register labels, and semantic claims.
Keeps most observations tied to concrete artifact pages or document slugs.
Breaks ordinary neutral prose with strong internal labels such as CONFIRMED, SUPPORTED, PARTIAL MISS, and NEW.
concerns: The file is very dense and mixes raw evidence, interpretation, prediction verdicts, and future decomposition work in one ledger.
Some statuses are sampled or probed while surrounding synthesis language can read as broad unless the local status labels are kept in view.
The final estate-vs-corpus section is only a placeholder.
notes: Briefing someone on this file: it is the run ledger that turns a ConceptV document corpus into named design substrates, observed rules, and judged prediction outcomes.
questions: What exact downstream files receive the decomposed token, registry entry, rule, and invariant records?
Which unread pages in sampled or probed documents remain outside this ledger's direct evidence base?
What criteria decide when a finding becomes an organism, atom, rule, invariant, or suggested decomposition?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file's behavior: it acts as a persistent evidence-first ledger for the ingestion run and records findings before downstream decomposition.
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve the ingestion run's memory, make every observed substrate traceable to source pages, and prepare findings for later decomposition into a fused design system without losing provenance.
novelty: The file treats presentations, product brochures, web mockups, and product UI screenshots as one evidentiary corpus for extracting reusable design grammar, including motion, register, shape, colour, and sequence laws.
contribution: Contributes a source-anchored ledger of ConceptV design substrates, observed organisms and atoms, register behaviors, presentation structures, and prediction verdicts that a fused system would keep as provenance-rich interpretive evidence.
summary_for_embedding: HARVEST-3.md is a dense evidence ledger for a 2026-06-12 full-corpus ConceptV ingestion run, cataloguing document statuses, page-cited design substrates, product and presentation findings, and judged prediction outcomes.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for preserving ingestion evidence first, then translating observed ConceptV artifacts into named design substrates, rules, questions, and downstream decomposition candidates.
